title: Apparatuses and methods for managing subsequent conditional pscell addition conditions
abstract: Example embodiments enable managing subsequent conditional PScell additions upon a secondary cell group release. A master node may be configured to send, to a user node, at least one execution condition for a conditional primary secondary cell, PScell, addition, CPA, to be applied by the user node for evaluation of a subsequent conditional PScell addition or change, SCPAC, condition for CPA execution to be performed after a secondary cell group, SCG, release, and wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is configured to replace at least one execution condition configured to the user node for evaluation of the SCPAC condition for a conditional PScell change, CPC. Apparatuses, methods, and computer programs are disclosed.
claims:
Claims 1 . A master node , comprising : at least one processor ; and at least one memory including instructions which, when executed by the at least one proces sor, cause the master node at least to : send, to a user node , at least one execution condition for a conditional primary secondary cell , PScell , addition, CPA, to be applied by the user node for evaluation of a subsequent conditional PScell addition or change , SCPAC, condition for execution of CPA to be performed after a secondary cell group, SCG, release , and wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is configured to replace at least one execution condition configured to the user node for evaluation of the SCPAC condition for a conditional PScell change , CPC . 2 . The master node of claim 1 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA comprises at least one A4 event condition mapped to a master cell group, MCG, configuration, wherein the at least one A4 event condition is defined for candidate SCPAC configurations . 3 . The master node of claim 2 , wherein the at least one execution condition comprises one A4 event condition common for all candidate SCPAC configurations . 4 . The master node of claim 2 , wherein the at least one execution condition comprises A4 event conditions speci fic for each candidate SCPAC configuration . 5 . The master node of claim 1 , wherein the at least 

one execution condition comprises instructions to maintain measurement configurations associated with the released SCG and SCPAC condition evaluation wherein the instructions instruct the user node to continue , after the SCG release , SCPAC condition evaluation based on at least one of A3 event condition or A5 event condition of the CPC with measurement configurations associated with the released SCG . 6 . The master node of any of claims 1 to 4 , wherein the at least one memory comprises instructions which, when executed by the at least one proces sor, cause the master node to : send an indication to deactivate the at least one execution condition; detect a need for a SCG release ; and send a reconfiguration message for the SCG release comprising an indication to the user node to activate the at least one execution condition . 7 . The master node of any of claims 1 to 5 , wherein the at least one execution condition is sent in a radio resource control , RRC, reconfiguration message for the SCG release . 8 . The master node of any of claims 1 to 6 , wherein the at least one execution condition is sent in a radio resource control , RRC, reconfiguration message including at least one of an initial conditional PScell change , CPC , condition configuration or a subsequent CPC condition configuration . 9 . The master node of any preceding claim, wherein 

the at least one memory further comprises instructions which, when executed by the at least one proces sor, cause the master node to : send instructions to the user node to suspend evaluation of the at least one execution condition for the released SCG . 10 . The master node of claim 9 , wherein the instructions sent to the user node comprise instructions to suspend the evaluation of the at least one execution condition for the released SCG for a certain period of time from the SCG release . 11 . The master node of claim 9 , wherein the instructions sent to the user node comprise instructions to suspend the evaluation of the at least one execution condition for the released SCG until the user node executes at least one conditional PCell addition . 12 . A user node , comprising : at least one processor ; and at least one memory including instructions which, when executed by the at least one processor , cause the user node at least to : receive , from a master node , at least one execution condition for a conditional primary secondary cell , PScell , addition, CPA, to be applied by the user node for evaluation of a subsequent conditional PScell addition or change , SCPAC, condition for execution of CPA to be performed after a secondary cell group, SCG, release, wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is configured to replace at least one execution condition configured to the user node for evaluation of the SCPAC condition for a conditional PScell change , CPC . 13 . The user node of claim 12 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA comprises at least one A4 event condition mapped to a master cell group, MCG, configuration, wherein the at least one A4 event condition is defined for candidate SCPAC configurations . 14 . The user of claim 13 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA comprises one A4 event condition common for all candidate SCPAC configurations . 15 . The user node of claim 13 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA comprises A4 event conditions speci fic for each candidate SCPAC configuration . 16 . The user node of any of claims 12 to 15 , wherein the at least one memory comprises instructions which, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the user node to : receive , from the master node , a reconfiguration message for SCG release ; perform the SCG release , comprising : releasing SCG configuration; releasing measurement configuration of at least one of an initial CPC condition or a subsequent CPC condition; releasing at least one of the CPC condition or the subsequent CPC condition; wherein the user node is caused to : evaluate the SCPAC condition based on the at least one execution condition for CPA; and after the SCG release , execute CPA based on the evaluating . 17 . The user node of claim 16 , wherein the at least 

one memory comprises instructions which, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the user node to : receive , from the master node , the at least one execution condition for CPA with an indication to deactivate the at least one execution condition for CPA; store the at least one execution condition in a deactivated state ; and receive , from the master node , the reconfiguration message for the SCG release with an indication to activate the at least one execution condition for CPA. 18 . The user node of claim 12 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA comprises instructions to maintain measurement configurations associated with the released SCG and SCPAC condition evaluation wherein the instructions instruct the user node to continue , after the SCG release , SCPAC condition evaluation based on the at least one of A3 event condition or A5 event condition of the CPC with measurement configurations associated with the released SCG . 19 . The user node of any of claims 12 to 18 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is received in a radio resource control , RRC , reconfiguration message including at least one of an initial CPC condition configuration or a subsequent CPC condition configuration . 20 . The user node of any of claims 12 to 18 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is received in a RRC reconfiguration message for the SCG release . 21 . The user node of any of claims 12 to 20 , wherein 

the at least one memory further comprises instructions which, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the user node to : receive instructions from the master node to suspend evaluation of the at least one execution condition for CPA for the released SCG; and suspend the evaluation of the at least one execution condition for CPA for the released SCG according to the instructions . 22 . The user node of claim 21 , wherein the instructions received from the master node comprise instructions to suspend the evaluation of the at least one execution condition for CPA for the released SCG for a certain period of time from the SCG release . 23 . The user node of claim 21 , wherein the instructions received from the master node comprise instructions to suspend the evaluation of the at least one execution condition for CPA for the released SCG until the user node executes at least one conditional PCell addition . 24 . The user node of any of claims 18 to 23 , wherein the at least one memory comprises instruction which, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the user node to : receive , from the master node , a reconfiguration message for SCG release before an initial conditional PScell change has been executed; perform the SCG release , comprising : releasing SCG configuration; maintaining measurement configurations of initial CPC conditions ; and maintaining initial CPC conditions ; 

 wherein the user node is caused to : update the initial CPC conditions for CPA, wherein the initial CPC conditions are evaluated for candidate SCPAC configurations with released PScell measurements as serving PScell measurements ; and upon or after the SCG release , perform evaluation for the conditional PScell addition execution based on the update . 25 . The user node of any of claims 18 to 23 , wherein the at least one memory comprises instruction which, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the user node to : receive , from the master node , a reconfiguration message for SCG release after an initial conditional PScell change has been executed; perform the SCG release , comprising : maintaining SCG configuration; maintaining measurement configurations of subsequent conditional PScell change , CPC , conditions ; maintaining subsequent CPC conditions ; and wherein the user node is caused to : update the subsequent CPC conditions for CPA, wherein the subsequent CPC conditions are evaluated for candidate SCPAC configurations with released PScell measurements as serving PScell measurements ; and upon or after the SCG release , perform evaluation for the conditional PScell addition execution based on the update . 26 . A method to be carried out by a master node , comprising : sending, to a user node , at least one execution condition for a conditional primary secondary cell , PScell , 

addition, CPA, to be applied by the user node for evaluation of a subsequent conditional PScell addition or change , SCPAC, condition for execution of CPA to be performed after a secondary cell group, SCG, release , and wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is configured to replace at least one execution condition configured to the user node for evaluation of the SCPAC condition for a conditional PScell change , CPC . 27 . The method of claim 26 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA comprises at least one A4 event condition mapped to a master cell group, MCG, configuration, wherein the at least one A4 event condition is defined for candidate SCPAC configurations . 28 . The method of claim 27 , wherein the at least one execution condition comprises one A4 event condition common for all candidate SCPAC configurations . 29 . The method of claim 27 , wherein the at least one execution condition comprises A4 event conditions speci fic for each candidate SCPAC configuration . 30 . The method of claim 26 , wherein the at least one execution condition comprises instructions to maintain measurement configurations associated with the released SCG and SCPAC condition evaluation wherein the instructions instruct the user node to continue , after the SCG release , SCPAC condition evaluation based on at least one of A3 event condition or A5 event condition of the CPC with measurement configurations associated with the released SCG . 31 . The method of any of claims 26 to 29 , comprising : 

 sending an indication to deactivate the at least one execution condition; detecting a need for a SCG release ; and sending a reconfiguration message for the SCG release comprising an indication to the user node to activate the at least one execution condition . 32 . The method of any of claims 26 to 30 , wherein the at least one execution condition is sent in a radio resource control , RRC, reconfiguration message for the SCG release . 33 . The method of any of claims 26 to 31 , wherein the at least one execution condition is sent in a radio resource control , RRC, reconfiguration message including at least one of an initial conditional PScell change , CPC, condition configuration or a subsequent CPC condition configuration . 34 . The method of any of claims 26 to 33 , comprising : sending instructions to the user node to suspend evaluation of the at least one execution condition for the released SCG . 35 . The method of claim 34 , wherein the instructions sent to the user node comprise instructions to suspend the evaluation of the at least one execution condition for the released SCG for a certain period of time from the SCG release . 36 . The method of claim 34 , wherein the instructions sent to the user node comprise instructions to suspend the evaluation of the at least one execution condition for the released SCG until the user node executes at least one conditional PCell addition . 37 . A method to be carried out by a user node , comprising : receiving, from a master node , at least one execution condition for a conditional primary secondary cell , PScell , addition, CPA, to be applied by the user node for evaluation of a subsequent conditional PScell addition or change , SCPAC, condition for execution of CPA to be performed after a secondary cell group, SCG, release , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is configured to replace at least one execution condition configured to the user node for evaluation of the SCPAC condition for a conditional PScell change , CPC . 38 . The method of claim 37 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA comprise at least one A4 event condition mapped to a master cell group, MCG, configuration, wherein the at least one A4 event condition is defined for candidate SCPAC configurations . 39 . The method of claim 38 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA comprises one A4 event condition common for all candidate SCPAC configurations . 40 . The method of claim 38 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA comprises A4 event conditions speci fic for each candidate SCPAC configuration . 41 . The method of any of claims 37 to 40 , comprising : receiving, from the master node , a reconfiguration message for SCG release ; performing the SCG release , comprising : releasing SCG configuration; releasing measurement configuration of at least one of an initial CPC condition or a subsequent CPC condition; 

 releasing at least one of the CPC condition or the subsequent CPC condition; evaluating the SCPAC condition based on the at least one execution condition for CPA; and after the SCG release , executing CPA based on the evaluating . 42 . The method of claim 41 , comprising : receiving, from the master node , the at least one execution condition for CPA with an indication to deactivate the at least one execution condition for CPA for execution of CPA; storing the at least one execution condition in a deactivated state ; and receiving, from the master node , the reconfiguration message for the SCG release with an indication to activate the at least one execution condition for CPA for execution of CPA. 43 . The method of claim 37 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA comprises instructions to maintain measurement configurations associated with the released SCG and SCPAC condition evaluation wherein the instructions instruct the user node to continue , after the SCG release , SCPAC condition evaluation based on the at least one of A3 event condition or A5 event condition of the CPC with measurement configurations associated with the released SCG . 44 . The method of any of claims 37 to 43 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is received in a radio resource control , RRC , reconfiguration message including at least one of an initial CPC condition configuration or a subsequent CPC condition configuration . 45 . The method of any of claims 37 to 43 , wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is received in a RRC reconfiguration message for the SCG release . 46 . The method of any of claims 37 to 45 , comprising : receiving instructions from the master node to suspend evaluation of the at least one execution condition for CPA for the released SCG; and suspending the evaluation of the at least one execution condition for CPA for the released SCG according to the instructions . 47 . The method of claim 46 , wherein the instructions received from the master node comprise instructions to suspend the evaluation of the at least one execution condition for CPA for the released SCG for a certain period of time from the SCG release . 48 . The method of claim 46 , wherein the instructions received from the master node comprise instructions to suspend the evaluation of the at least one execution condition for CPA for the released SCG until the user node executes at least one conditional PCell addition . 49 . configurations with released PScell measurements as serving PScell measurements ; and upon or after the SCG release , performing evaluation for the conditional PScell addition execution based on the update . 50 . The method of any of claims 37 to 48 , comprising : receiving, from the master node , a reconfiguration message for SCG release after an initial conditional PScell change has been executed; performing the SCG release , comprising : maintaining SCG configuration, maintaining measurement configurations of subsequent conditional PScell change , CPC , conditions ; maintaining subsequent CPC conditions ; and updating the subsequent CPC conditions for CPA, wherein the subsequent CPC conditions are evaluated for candidate SCPAC configurations with released PScell measurements as serving PScell measurements ; and upon or after the SCG release , performing evaluation for the conditional PScell addition execution based on the update . 51 . An apparatus comprising at least means for sending, to a user node , at least one execution condition for a conditional primary secondary cell , PScell , addition, CPA, to be applied by the user node for evaluation of a subsequent conditional PScell addition or change , SCPAC, condition evaluation for execution of CPA to be performed after a secondary cell group, SCG, release , and wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is configured to replace at least one execution condition configured to the user node for evaluation of the SCPAC condition for a conditional PScell change , CPC . 52 . An apparatus comprising at least means for receiving, from a master node , at least one execution condition for a conditional primary secondary cell , PScell , addition, CPA, to be applied by the user node for evaluation of a subsequent conditional PScell addition or change , SCPAC, condition evaluation for execution of CPA to be performed after a secondary cell group, SCG, release , and wherein the at least one execution condition for CPA is configured to replace at least one execution condition configured to the user node for evaluation of the SCPAC condition for a conditional PScell change , CPC . 53 . A computer readable medium comprising instructions which, when executed by an apparatus , cause the apparatus to perform at least the method of any of claims 26 to 36 . 54 . A computer readable medium comprising instructions which, when executed by an apparatus cause the apparatus to perform at least the method of any of claims 37 to 50 .
